county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Vernon holds records from 1819. Mount Vernon was an important stop on the road between Vincennes and St. Louis. Abraham Lincoln practiced law in the Mount Vernon courthouse on the circuit. Jefferson County Historical Society. Appellate Court records (5th District) housed in Mount Vernon. Created from Edwards and White — check both for pre-1819 records. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Coal mining heritage. Italian immigrant community in mining towns.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1819. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Circuit Clerk, Mount Vernon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1819. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Circuit Clerk, Mount Vernon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1819. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Jefferson County Clerk, Mount Vernon / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1819. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Jefferson County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

source_profiles.source_name: Oil and Gas Lease Records — Jefferson County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease agreements recorded in county deed books. Illinois oil fields (particularly in southeastern Illinois) generated extensive lease and mineral rights records from the early 1900s onward. These records identify landowners and track property transactions.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved in county deed records

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jerseyville holds records from 1839. Jersey County is at the confluence of the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers. Jerseyville attracted New England settlers — strong Congregational church records. Check Greene County for pre-1839 records. Jersey County Historical Society. Pere Marquette State Park area — French exploration heritage. Mississippi River commerce records. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Agricultural county with strong livestock and dairy records. Grafton on the rivers is a historic ferry town.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1839. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1839. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives

source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Galena holds records from 1827. Galena was once the wealthiest city in Illinois due to lead mining — Galena Lead District records document miners (many from Cornwall, England, and the Upper South). Ulysses S. Grant's pre-Civil War home is in Galena. Jo Daviess County attracted Cornish, Irish, and German miners. Galena & Chicago Union Railroad was Illinois' first railroad. Jo Daviess County Historical Society. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Check Henry and Putnam counties for pre-1827 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1827. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Vienna holds records from 1812, one of Illinois' oldest counties. Johnson County is in the Shawnee Hills — early settlers came from the Upland South. Vienna is a small county seat with strong southern Illinois heritage. Tunnel Hill area. Johnson County Genealogical and Historical Society. Created from Randolph — check Randolph for earlier records. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Strong Baptist church records. Trail of Tears route passed through Johnson County — Cherokee removal records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1812. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Geneva holds records from 1836. Kane County spans from Fox River communities (Geneva, St. Charles, Batavia, Aurora, Elgin) with diverse settlement. Aurora attracted a large Hispanic community in the 20th century. Elgin was known for the Elgin National Watch Company — factory employment records. Batavia had significant Scandinavian settlement. Kane County Forest Preserve holdings. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Check LaSalle County for pre-1836 records. Kane County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: African American Church and Community Records — Kane County
source_profiles.repository: Vivian G. Harsh Research Collection (Chicago Public Library) / local churches and historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: African American church records (AME, Baptist, COGIC), fraternal organization records, and community newspapers. The Great Migration (1910-1970) brought hundreds of thousands of African Americans from the South to Illinois, particularly Chicago and industrial cities. Chicago Defender newspaper is a critical source.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Vivian G. Harsh Collection at Chicago Public Library is the largest African American history collection in the Midwest

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Kankakee holds records from 1853. Kankakee County had a notable French-Canadian community — Bourbonnais was settled by French Canadians from Quebec in the 1830s. Olivet Nazarene University (1907) in Bourbonnais. Kankakee River area. Kankakee County Historical Society. Check Iroquois and Will counties for pre-1853 records. IRAD at Illinois State University. French-Canadian parish records at Maternity BVM Church in Bourbonnais are important. Railroad town heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1853. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Yorkville holds records from 1841. Kendall County is one of the fastest-growing counties in Illinois (suburban Chicago expansion). Yorkville is on the Fox River. Check LaSalle and Kane counties for pre-1841 records. Kendall County Historical Society in Yorkville. Lyon Farm and Village preserves local heritage. Early settlers from New England and New York. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Fox River communities. Strong Congregational and Methodist church records from early settlement.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Galesburg holds records from 1825. Knox College (1837) in Galesburg — site of a Lincoln-Douglas debate (1858). The college archives hold extensive local collections. Galesburg was founded as a manual labor college colony by George Washington Gale. Carl Sandburg was born in Galesburg (1878). Knox County had early abolitionist activity. Swedish immigrants settled in Galesburg area — Augustana Lutheran records. Check Fulton County for pre-1825 records. IRAD at Western Illinois University.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Knox County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

source_profiles.repository: Knox County Circuit Clerk, Galesburg / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Swedish American Church Records — Knox County
source_profiles.repository: Swenson Swedish Immigration Research Center, Rock Island / Augustana Heritage Association
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Augustana Lutheran and other Swedish denomination church records — baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Bishop Hill Colony records (Henry County). Swenson Center at Augustana College holds the largest Swedish American research collection in the U.S.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Swenson Center has extensive microfilm and original records; many indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Ottawa holds records from 1831. LaSalle County was the site of the first Lincoln-Douglas debate (1858) in Ottawa. Illinois & Michigan Canal terminus at LaSalle — canal construction records document Irish workers. Starved Rock State Park area — French colonial heritage (Fort St. Louis). Coal mining in Streator and surrounding towns drew Italian, Lithuanian, and Eastern European immigrants. LaSalle County Historical Society. IRAD at Illinois State University. Silica sand mining records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1831. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: Illinois & Michigan Canal Records — LaSalle County
source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / I&M Canal National Heritage Corridor
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land sales, and worker rolls. The I&M Canal (1836-1848) connected Lake Michigan to the Illinois River. Canal construction employed thousands of Irish immigrant laborers. Canal land grant records document property sales along the corridor.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Illinois State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Waukegan holds records from 1839. Lake County is in the Chicago suburban collar — diverse communities from wealthy North Shore (Lake Forest, Highland Park) to industrial Waukegan and North Chicago. Fort Sheridan military post records. Great Lakes Naval Training Station (established 1911) — military personnel records. Lake County Discovery Museum holds local collections. Check McHenry County for pre-1839 records. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Significant Jewish community on the North Shore.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
